cards.grammarNote: avoir verb: j'ai + mangé. au = à + le (contraction). -ER verbs form the past participle by replacing -er with -é: manger → mangé.
avoir verb: nous avons + commandé. du vin = partitive article (some wine). The past participle commandé uses the regular -ER pattern.
Direct object pronoun l' (le/la before vowel) placed before the auxiliary avoir in passé composé. goûter → goûté (regular -ER verb).
boire has an irregular past participle: bu. Uses avoir as auxiliary.
aller uses être as auxiliary. Masculine plural subject adds -s to the participle: allés.
-IR verb: choisir → choisi. Uses avoir as auxiliary.
partir uses être as auxiliary. Feminine subject: partie (add -e). du = de + le (contraction).

cards.back: Je n'ai pas bu de vin
cards.grammarNote: Negation ne...pas wraps the auxiliary: je n'ai pas + past participle. After negation, du/de la/des → de: pas de vin.
on (informal 'we') takes the same verb form as il/elle. bien (well) is placed between the auxiliary and the past participle in conversational French.
